24/7 EMERGENCY CARE

Pediatric Emergency Care

Expert pediatric emergency services for critically ill and injured children. With 18+ years of experience and UK training, Dr. Sanjay D Swamy provides immediate, life-saving care when your child needs it most.

24/7 Always Available
<5min Response Time
10K+ Emergencies Handled
Emergency: Call Now Get Directions

Immediate Response

Critical cases seen within minutes

Advanced Life Support

Pediatric resuscitation expertise

Specialist On-Call

Pediatric intensivist available 24/7

ABOUT EMERGENCY CARE

When Every Second Counts

Our pediatric emergency department is designed specifically for children, with specialized equipment, trained staff, and a child-friendly environment to provide the best possible care during emergencies.

Pediatric emergencies require specialized knowledge and equipment. Children are not small adults - they have unique physiological responses, different medication dosages, and special emotional needs during medical crises. Our emergency department is designed specifically for pediatric care.

Led by Dr. Sanjay D Swamy, a UK-trained pediatric intensivist with 18+ years of experience, our emergency team includes pediatric specialists, trained nurses, and support staff available around the clock. We use child-sized equipment, age-appropriate pain management, and create a calming environment.

Our rapid triage system ensures critical cases receive immediate attention. We coordinate seamlessly with PICU and NICU when intensive care is needed, providing continuity of care from emergency to recovery.

Why Choose Our Emergency Care

  • 18+ years emergency expertise
  • UK-trained pediatric specialist
  • 24/7 doctor availability
  • Advanced life support ready
  • Child-friendly environment
  • Rapid triage system

OUR CAPABILITIES

Comprehensive Emergency Services

Our emergency department offers a full spectrum of pediatric emergency services with advanced monitoring, life support, and specialized treatments.

24/7 Emergency Services

Round-the-clock pediatric emergency care with immediate triage and treatment for all childhood emergencies requiring urgent attention.

Acute Illness Management

Rapid assessment and treatment of sudden onset illnesses including high fever, severe infections, and respiratory distress in children.

Trauma & Injury Care

Expert management of pediatric trauma including fractures, head injuries, burns, and accidental injuries with child-friendly approach.

Poison Control

Immediate response to accidental poisoning and toxic ingestions with specialized pediatric toxicology protocols and rapid intervention.

Allergic Emergencies

Rapid treatment of severe allergic reactions, anaphylaxis, and acute asthma attacks with advanced life support capabilities.

Seizure Management

Emergency care for convulsions and seizures with immediate stabilization, continuous monitoring, and comprehensive neurological assessment.

Child Emergency? Call Us Immediately

24/7 pediatric emergency care available. Don't wait.

Call Now Get Directions

YOUR CHILD'S EMERGENCY SPECIALIST

Dr. Sanjay D Swamy

Dr. Sanjay D Swamy is a Chief Paediatric Intensivist with 18+ years of experience in managing pediatric emergencies. His advanced training at Birmingham Children's Hospital, UK, equipped him with expertise in handling the most critical pediatric cases.

18+
Years Experience
Dedicated career in pediatric emergency and intensive care with thousands of lives saved.
UK
Advanced Training
Fellowship at Birmingham Children's Hospital with world-class emergency care practices.

ADVANCED CAPABILITIES

Emergency Facilities

Our emergency department is equipped with state-of-the-art facilities and specialized equipment for comprehensive pediatric emergency care.

01

Rapid Response Team

Dedicated pediatric emergency team trained in advanced life support available 24/7 for immediate intervention.

02

Advanced Diagnostics

On-site X-ray, ultrasound, and CT scan facilities for quick and accurate diagnosis in emergency situations.

03

Pediatric Resuscitation

Fully equipped resuscitation bay with age-appropriate equipment and medications for pediatric emergencies.

04

Short Stay Unit

Observation unit for children requiring monitoring after emergency treatment before safe discharge home.

EMERGENCIES WE HANDLE

Pediatric Emergencies

Our team is experienced in managing a wide range of pediatric emergencies requiring immediate medical attention.

High Fever & Febrile Seizures
Severe Respiratory Distress
Acute Asthma Attacks
Severe Allergic Reactions
Head Injuries
Fractures & Dislocations
Burns & Scalds
Accidental Poisoning
Severe Dehydration
Drowning Incidents
Foreign Body Ingestion
Severe Abdominal Pain
Convulsions & Seizures
Diabetic Emergencies
Animal Bites
Electric Shock

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

Questions About Emergency Care

When should I bring my child to the emergency room?

Seek emergency care for difficulty breathing, high fever in infants under 3 months, severe allergic reactions, head injuries with loss of consciousness, seizures, severe bleeding, signs of dehydration, poisoning, or any condition where your child appears very ill or unresponsive.

What should I bring to the emergency room?

Bring your child's health card, vaccination records, list of current medications, any relevant medical history, and if poisoning is suspected, bring the container or substance. Also bring comfort items like a favorite toy to help calm your child.

How is the emergency department different for children?

Our pediatric emergency department is specifically designed for children with child-sized equipment, child-friendly environment, and staff specially trained in pediatric emergencies. We understand children's unique medical needs and emotional responses.

What happens when we arrive at the emergency room?

Upon arrival, a triage nurse will quickly assess your child's condition and assign a priority level. Critical cases are seen immediately. You'll then be taken to an examination room where the emergency pediatrician will evaluate and treat your child.

Can I stay with my child during treatment?

Yes, parents are encouraged to stay with their child throughout most emergency procedures. Your presence provides comfort and helps medical staff gather important information. In rare cases during certain procedures, we may ask you to step out briefly.

What if my child needs to be admitted after emergency care?

If your child requires admission, our emergency team will coordinate with the appropriate department (pediatric ward, PICU, or NICU) for seamless transfer. We ensure continuity of care and keep you informed throughout the process.
